)>z6Built-in template tests used with the ``is`` operator.

value
returnc
z#Return true if the variable is odd.
0/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/jinja2/tests.py
test_odd
z$Return true if the variable is even.r
	test_even
numr	
z-Check if a variable is divisible by a number.r
test_divisibleby
Return true if the variable is defined:
    .. sourcecode:: jinja
        {% if variable is defined %}
            value of variable: {{ variable }}
        {% else %}
            variable is not defined
        {% endif %}
    See the :func:`default` filter for a simple way to set undefined
    variables.
    
isinstancer
test_defined
z-Like :func:`defined` but the other way round.r
test_undefined.
envr
Check if a filter exists by name. Useful if a filter may be
    optionally available.
    .. code-block:: jinja
        {% if 'markdown' is filter %}
            {{ value | markdown }}
        {% else %}
            {{ value }}
        {% endif %}
    .. versionadded:: 3.0
    )
filters
test_filter3
Check if a test exists by name. Useful if a test may be
    optionally available.
    .. code-block:: jinja
        {% if 'loud' is test %}
            {% if value is loud %}
                {{ value|upper }}
            {% else %}
                {{ value|lower }}
            {% endif %}
        {% else %}
            {{ value }}
        {% endif %}
    .. versionadded:: 3.0
    )
testsr
	test_testE

	test_none[

z^Return true if the variable is a sequence. Sequences are variables
    that are iterable.

test_sequence
otherr	
Check if an object points to the same memory address than another
    object:
    .. sourcecode:: jinja
        {% if foo.attribute is sameas false %}
            the foo attribute really is the `False` singleton
        {% endif %}
    r
